title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Type Recall
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs express 5 routing, path syntax, parameters, and dispatch from bounded code and request evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.
Treat decoded string or wildcard-array parameters as untrusted input and validate identifier grammar, length, canonical form, and existence separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Express5 Path Syntax · changed case · Review Express5 Path Syntax
steps.instruction:
review case — A review team handles case 5948 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
